meta_title: For Freelancers: SaaS starter kit to Solve can't deploy apps (Intermediate Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS starter kit and overcome can't deploy apps. A Intermediate-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: SaaS Starter Kit: Deploy Faster as a Freelancer
content.seo_description: Stop struggling with deployment. Use this intermediate SaaS starter kit checklist with Antigravity and Claude Code to ship client projects in record time.
content.answer_block: Stop manually configuring infrastructure. By leveraging a battle-tested SaaS starter kit, you unify your authentication, database, and deployment pipelines into a single, repeatable workflow. Using Antigravity for architecture and Claude Code for rapid implementation, you can transition from local code to a live, production-ready URL in under thirty minutes.
content.intro:
The clock is ticking, your client is waiting, and your terminal is mocking you with a wall of red error logs. We have all been there: trapped in the 'deployment purgatory' where the code works on your machine but vanishes into the void the moment you try to push to production. It is the silent killer of freelance momentum and the fastest way to erode client trust.

But the era of manual infrastructure hell is over. By adopting a high-velocity SaaS starter kit, you stop reinventing the wheel and start shipping value. This guide provides the blueprint to automate your DevOps, leverage AI-driven coding agents, and ensure that every project you touch deploys with the reliability of a senior-led engineering team.

steps.title: Standardize the Boilerplate
steps.description: Select a production-grade stack (Next.js, Supabase, Tailwind) and clone your base starter kit to ensure consistent project architecture.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Configure a project scaffold with Next.js 14, Supabase Auth, and Stripe integration using the standard modular architecture pattern.

steps.title: Automate Infrastructure Provisioning
steps.description: Use Antigravity to define your cloud environment, ensuring your database and environment variables are synced automatically.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate infrastructure-as-code for Vercel deployment with automated database migrations and environment variable injection.

steps.title: Implement AI-Assisted Feature Injection
steps.description: Use Claude Code to build specific client features while adhering to the existing codebase patterns.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Build a user dashboard component that fetches data from the Supabase client and follows the project's existing design system.

steps.title: Configure CI/CD Pipelines
steps.description: Lock down your deployment cycle by setting up automated linting and testing triggers on every push to your main branch.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a GitHub Actions workflow that runs linting, testing, and deployment to Vercel only on successful builds.

steps.title: Final Production Smoke Test
steps.description: Perform a final audit of the production environment to ensure all API keys and redirects are correctly mapped.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Review the current repository for hardcoded secrets and verify that all necessary environment variables are defined in the production config.

pro_tips: Always define your environment variables in a .env.example file to prevent deployment failure for teammates.
Use 'dry-run' flags in your deployment commands to catch configuration errors before they hit your cloud provider.
Keep your database schema versioned in a /migrations folder to ensure production parity across development environments.
Configure a 'staging' branch that auto-deploys to a unique URL for client review before the production push.
Use Claude Code to write unit tests for your API routes; it is faster than manual verification and prevents regression bugs.
content.next_steps: Once your first deployment is live, audit your build times and create a custom template repository to reuse this setup for your next three client projects.
content.conversion_block: You now have the technical edge to stop debugging infrastructure and start scaling your freelance revenue. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
